title: Wholemount Edu Staining (Zebrafish Larvae)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3kqal","text":"Methods for evaluating cell proliferation are important for research in the field of developmental biology, cancer biology and cell biology. Recent years, Click-iT EdU Assays by Invitrogen is gaining popularity due to its fast staining speed, mild tissue treatment condition, reproducibility and specificity. However, the standard kit from Invitrogen is designed for tissue cultured cells, hence the manufactory provided protocol is optimized for cells cultured on coverslips. EdU staining became a popular method to evaluate cell proliferation in vivo in zebrafish embryos. However, the protocols that we have tested in the past often yield inconsistent results due to variable EdU incorporation efficiency. Here we describe an EdU staining protocol that we optimized for zebrafish larvae, which incorporated a crucial EdU injection step. Our protocol was used successfully by many new students in training, without any issues. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Inject 2nl bolus of 500uM EdU into the yolk of embryos Reagents for EdU staining can be purchased from - https://www.thermofisher.com/uk/en/home/references/protocols/cell-and-tissue-analysis/protocols/click-it-plus-edu-imaging-protocol.html embryos were immobilized in 3% methylcellulose in 0.3 x DANEAU's solution (or E3) and returned to induction solution after injection.
Incubate at 28.5°C in the dark for 2.5 hours (this can be between 2 -3 hours, but be consistent between experiments; we have done 2 hours)
Wash in 0.1% PBT ( 5min , RT shaking) 30 rpm, 25 °C, 5 minutes, repeat 3 times
Wash in 3% BSA, Dilute in PBT ( 5min , RT shaking) -grams/100ml, i.e. 3g/100ml, 300mg in 10ml. 30 rpm, 25 °C,
Block in 3% BSA, Dilute in PBT ( 1h , shaking) 30 rpm, 25 °C, 1 hour
Incubate in staining solution (as per kit protocol) – we use 250ul per sample (10-15 larvae) 30 min , 30 rpm, 25 °C, 30 minutes 250 µL, per 10-15 larvae
Wash in PBT ( 5min , RT shaking x3) 30 rpm, 25 °C, 5 minutes, X3
Wash in 5% Goat Serum, Dilute in PBT ( 5min , RT shaking) 30 rpm, 25 °C, 5 minutes
Block in 5% Goat Serum, Dilute in PBT ( at least 2h , RT shaking) 30 rpm, 25 °C, 2 hours

Incubate with anti-GFP Primary Ab 1:200, dilute in 5% Goat Serum in PBT (O/N, +4 °C shaking) 30 rpm, 4 °C, 16 hours, Over night 250 µL, 10-30 larvae GFP (D5.1)XP Rabbit mAB (Cell Signalling Technology 2956)
Wash in PBT ( 25-30 min , RT shaking) x 8-10 à >4 hours in total!!! 30 rpm, 25 °C, 30 minutes, X10

Incubate with Secondary Ab [] 1:250, Dilute in 3% Goat Serum in PBT (overnight at +4 °C -- prefered) 30 rpm, 4 °C, 16 hours, Or 25C 2 hours Alexa Fluor 488 goat anti-rabbit IgG (H+L) (Invitrogen A11008)] This step over night is preferred, however, 2 hours at RT also works.
Wash in PBT ( 15 min , RT shaking) x4 30 rpm, 25 °C, 15 minutes, X 4
Stain with Hoechst 33258 Dilute 1 in 1000 in PBT ( 30mins , RT shaking) 30 rpm, 25 °C, 30 minutes, in dark Or other nuclear stain such as DAPI
Wash in PBT (15mins, PBT) x4 30 rpm, 25 °C, 15 minutes, X 4
Mounting with cover-slip and glass slides.
Incubate in AF1 solution. https://www.cntech-labsupplies.co.uk/products/sample-preparation/film-forming-polymer-solutions Sample can be stored in the dark for few weeks
creator: Yi Feng
